WebSpace ABP rule probe

Drops a sample element for every rule shape we support, waits for the cosmetic shim to settle, then reports per row whether the target's computed display is none. Use this to verify which rule kinds your active filter lists actually catch.

BLOCKED rule fired — element hidden visible rule did not fire pending… waiting for shim to settle note host- or network-dependent

Cosmetic rules

Each row's target lives in a hidden stage element. The shim's early <style> tag (if it caught this rule) will set display: none !important on the target.

Rule shape Status Sample markup

Network rules (heuristic — read with care)

Loads a tiny resource from each domain. This section is best-effort: a failed load can mean the rule fired, the URL doesn't serve content, or a transient network error. Trust this section only when the control rows succeed. The cosmetic section above is the reliable end-to-end signal — sub-resource block status is better verified from the in-app Developer Tools → Network panel on a reload.

Rule Status Probe target

Page itself, for sanity

If the chrome below is hidden, your filter list is over-matching the test page itself — file a bug.

This paragraph should remain visible. [chrome marker]
